questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the average of the four bar values shown: $4, 6, 8, 10$.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='75' width='25' height='45'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='45' width='25' height='75'/></g><text x='55' y='138' font-size='8'>4, 6, 8, 10</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: 'By the time the rescue team arrived, the trapped miners ____ for nearly ten hours.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An action continuing up to a point in the past uses the past perfect continuous tense.

**Working.** The reference point ('the team arrived') is in the past, and the waiting was ongoing before it. This requires 'had been + verb-ing'.

**Answer.** had been waiting.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the right triangle shown, the legs are $6$ and $8$. Compute the hypotenuse.

figure.caption: Right triangle
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,130 160,130 40,50' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='40' y='118' width='12' height='12' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><text x='90' y='145' font-size='8'>8</text><text x='15' y='95' font-size='8'>6</text><text x='105' y='80' font-size='8'>hyp?</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at exactly $4{:}00$ is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angle $=30^\circ\times$ (hours between hands).

**Working.** At $4{:}00$ the hands are $4$ marks apart: $4\times 30^\circ = 120^\circ$.

**Answer.** $120$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Given 'All P are Q' and 'All Q are R' (with P non-empty), which of the following necessarily follow?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply transitivity and existential import of a non-empty set.

**Working.** P ⊆ Q ⊆ R gives 'All P are R'; since P is non-empty, 'Some R are P' holds; 'All Q are R' gives 'Some Q are R'. 'All R are P' is not implied (R may be larger).

**Answer.** All P are R; Some R are P; Some Q are R.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle is divided into four smaller triangles by joining the midpoints of its sides (shown). The total number of triangles is _____ .

figure.caption: A triangle with its three midpoints joined, forming four small triangles.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='80,15 20,120 140,120' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='67' x2='110' y2='67' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='67' x2='80' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='110' y1='67' x2='80' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count the small triangles and the original.

**Working.** Joining midpoints makes $4$ small triangles; with the original large triangle the total is $4+1=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A circle is inscribed in a square of side $10$ (figure). Compute the shaded area outside the circle but inside the square (use $\pi=3.1416$, two decimals).

figure.caption: Inscribed circle in a square
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='25' y='25' width='120' height='120' fill='#2563eb'/><circle cx='85' cy='85' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb'/><text x='30' y='168' font-size='8'>square 10, inscribed circle</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shaded $=$ square area $-$ circle area, with $r=5$.

**Working.**
$$A=10^2-3.1416(5)^2=100-78.54=21.46$$

**Answer.** $21.46$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average age of $30$ students is $12$ years. When the teacher's age is included, the average rises to $13$ years. The teacher's age is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total age = average times number of people.

**Working.** Students' total $=30\times 12=360$; with teacher $=31\times 13=403$. Teacher $=403-360=43$.

**Answer.** $43$ years.

questions.subject: Foundation Engineering
questions.stem: Immediate (elastic) settlement of a footing on saturated clay is computed using
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Immediate settlement uses $E_u$, $\nu=0.5$ (no volume change).

**Working.**
$E_u$, $\nu=0.5$.

**Answer.** Undrained $E_u$, $\nu=0.5$
